content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 11,
1992
Warren Slocum
County Clerk—Recorder
San Mateo County
401 Marshall Street
Redwood City,
CA
94063—1636
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-450
Dear Mr.
Slocuiu:
You have forwarded for our review a request from Peter
Bagatelos on behalf of the Heritage Site Plan Initiative,
Sponsored by Half Moon Bay Heritage Company,
Ltd.
(the
“Committee”), concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”) •1/
Mr.
Bagatelos has requested
that the Committee’s 21-day post-qualification campaign statement
be combined with the second pre—election campaign statement due in
connection with the November 3,
1992 election.
Section 84205 provides:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required to
be filed by this title.
According to Mr. Bagatelos’ letter, the Committee is required
under Section 84200.5(f) to file a post—qualification campaign
statement no later than October 28,
1992.
The Committee also is
required to file pre—election campaign statements in connection
with the November 3,
1992, election.
(Section 84200.7.)
As a
result, the Committee’s post-qualification campaiqu statement will
cover a period of four days.
Under these circumstances,
we
believe it is appropriate to combine the post-qualification
statement with the second pre—election statement.
Therefore, the
Committee may use the following filing schedule:
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
I
Sacramento, CA
95812

Warren Slocum

If you have any questions concerning this matter, please
contact me at

Sincerely,

Peter Bagatelos

sections.question: May the Committee's 21-day post-qualification campaign statement be combined with the second pre-election campaign statement due in connection with the November 3, 1992 election?
sections.conclusion: Under Section 84205, the Commission may permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements required to be filed. Given that the Committee's post-qualification statement would cover only a four-day period, the Commission believes it is appropriate to combine the post-qualification statement with the second pre-election statement. The Committee may follow the provided filing schedule with the combined statement due October 22, 1992, covering October 1-17, 1992.
sections.question_synthetic: The Heritage Site Plan Initiative Committee requested permission to combine its required post-qualification campaign statement (due October 28, 1992) with its second pre-election statement for the November 3, 1992 election, as the post-qualification statement would otherwise cover only four days.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approved the Committee's request to combine the post-qualification statement with the second pre-election statement, as the post-qualification statement would otherwise cover only four days, making the combination administratively appropriate under Section 84205.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear FPPC advice letter with explicit approval. Question and conclusion are clearly identifiable. Document includes a detailed filing schedule table. No sections explicitly labeled 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' but content is structured accordingly.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the Committee's 21-day post-qualification campaign statement be combined with the second pre-election campaign statement due in connection with the November 3, 1992 election?

CONCLUSION: Under Section 84205, the Commission may permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements required to be filed. Given that the Committee's post-qualification statement would cover only a four-day period, the Commission believes it is appropriate to combine the post-qualification statement with the second pre-election statement. The Committee may follow the provided filing schedule with the combined statement due October 22, 1992, covering October 1-17, 1992.

(the “Committee”), concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) •1/ Mr. Bagatelos has requested that the Committee’s 21-day post-qualification campaign statement be combined with the second pre—election campaign statement due in connection with the November 3, 1992 election. Section 84205 provides: The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title. According to Mr. Bagatelos’ letter, the Committee is required under Section 84200.5(f) to file a post—qualification campaign statement no later than October 28, 1992. The Committee also is required to file pre—election campaign statements in connection with the November 3, 1992, election. (Section 84200.7.) As a result, the Committee’s post-qualification campaiqu statement will cover a period of four days. Under these circumstances, we believe it is appropriate to combine the post-qualification statement with the second pre—election statement. Therefore, the Committee may use the following filing schedule: 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 I Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660 Warren Slocum Our File No. A-92-450 Page 2 Type of Statement Period Covered Filing Deadline 1st Pre—Election Statement in con nection with the November 3 election July 1 — Sept. 30 Oct. 5, 1992 Post—Qualif 1- cation Statement combined with the 2nd Pre—Election State ment in connection with the November 3 election Oct. 1 - Oct. 17 Oct. 22, 1992 Semi-Annual Statement Oct. 18 — Dec. 31 Jan. 31, 1993 If you have any questions concerning this matter, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel 2 M By: Carla Wardlow Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division cc: Peter Bagatelos
embedding.summary: The FPPC approved a request from the Heritage Site Plan Initiative Committee to combine its 21-day post-qualification campaign statement with its second pre-election campaign statement for the November 3, 1992 election, providing an approved filing schedule that consolidates these filings.
